name: Host Upload via Executa
description: Demonstrates the host/uploadFile reverse-RPC (inline / negotiate / confirm) by generating a local scratch file and persisting it to short-lived host storage — bytes go plugin→R2 directly, never through tools.invoke. Backed by the host.upload capability.
_comment: Publish metadata (slug/name/version/executa_type) is read by `anna-app apps publish`; the dev fields (tool_id/type/enabled) are read by `anna-app dev`. The `distribution` block is read by `anna-app executa publish` (invoked transitively by `apps push`/`apps publish`) and forwarded onto the server Executa row (distribution_type/executable_name/supports_protocol/capabilities/binary_urls) so the Agent can INSTALL this Executa — without it the tool is dev-only. `type:"binary"` + `binary_urls` ships a self-contained PyInstaller binary per platform; the URLs point at this repo's GitHub Release assets (tag `file-upload-via-executa-v<version>`), built by .github/workflows/anna-app-publish.yml. On `anna-app apps cut`, matrix-nexus mirrors these bytes into its own R2 (apps.anna.partners), content-addresses them by sha256, and pins binary_sha256 — so you do NOT need to host on R2 yourself or declare sha256 here (the mirror computes it). This dir is the `file-upload-via-executa` handle referenced by app.json#bundled_executas. host/uploadFile needs `anna-app dev` signed in (to mint an upload_token) but does NOT require `--storage aps`.
